description_long: This object is a detailed representation of a crustacean, specifically resembling a freshwater crayfish or lobster. Its body is segmented into a robust cephalothorax and a flexible abdomen, with a hard, dark exoskeleton that appears glossy and slightly textured. The front features two large, prominent claws (chelae) with serrated edges, used for defense and manipulating food. It has multiple jointed walking legs extending from the underside, and two long, slender antennae projecting forward from the head. The eyes are positioned on short stalks, providing a wide field of vision. The overall coloration is a deep brown to black, with lighter hues on the ventral side and joints.
description: A dark, glossy crustacean with large claws, segmented body, and long antennae, resembling a crayfish or lobster. Its exoskeleton is hard and textured, with multiple legs and stalked eyes.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the body, both claws, and the extended antennae.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the segmentation of the body, symmetry of the claws, and dorsal surface texture.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, displaying the curvature of the tail and the arrangement of the legs and claws.
description_view_3: Frontal view emphasizing the size and shape of the claws, the placement of the eyes on stalks, and the forward-projecting antennae.
